title: [lichen_l2.c:1654-1656] net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer timeout is fixed, not adaptive
description:
Completeness: Resource management category.

At line 1654-1656, net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer uses a fixed timeout from CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_RX_ALLOC_TIMEOUT_MS.

The existing finding project-LICHEN-tvfm.99 (referenced at line 1640) documents that memory pressure causes frame drops. However:

1. The timeout is static - no adaptive backoff under sustained pressure
2. No metrics/counter to observe allocation failures
3. No flow control signal back to the sender

For LoRa mesh, frame loss is expected and handled by upper layers. However, observability is missing - there's no way to know if packets are being dropped due to memory pressure vs other causes.

Consider adding a k_uptime_get() based throttle log (e.g., log at most once per minute) when allocation fails, or increment a statistic counter for monitoring.
close_reason: Consolidated with i1gk.62: added FUTURE comment documenting fixed timeout and potential for adaptive backoff/counters
